rationale: The cited paper is treated as one of the key closest predecessors, not just a background mention. The citing paper repeatedly uses it to frame the research gap (single-brand focus, short campaign window, partial self-reported exposure data) and explicitly states that the new study "picks up from where these studies left off." It also uses the cited study for conceptual setup (direct media-to-sales effects), empirical benchmarking of elasticity magnitudes, and even an input for ROI calculations (email cost). These roles show meaningful intellectual dependence and direct advancement of that prior line of work.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Danaher and Dagger (2013) by moving from a single-brand, short blitz setting to a multi-brand, multi-channel, two-year customer panel with automated exposure measures and cross-brand effects. It builds on the prior multimedia effectiveness framework while broadening scope, data richness, and managerial implications.
